Real Analysis

Intermediate Value Theorem

Intermediate Value Theorem

Let I=[a,b]I = [a, b]I=[a,b] be an interval and f:I→Rf: I \rightarrow \mathbb{R}f:I→R be continuous on III. If f(a)<0<f(b)f(a) < 0 < f(b)f(a)<0<f(b) or f(a)>0>f(b)f(a) > 0 > f(b)f(a)>0>f(b), then there exists a number c∈(a,b)c \in (a, b)c∈(a,b) such that f(c)=0f(c) = 0f(c)=0.

Proof: Assume f(a)<0<f(b)f(a) < 0 <f(b)f(a)<0<f(b)

Consider a+b2\frac{a+b}{2}2a+b​

If f(a+b2)<0f(\frac{a+b}{2})<0f(2a+b​)<0. Then we know

Let a2,=a+b2a_2, = \frac{a+b}{2}a2​,=2a+b​, b2=b1=bb_2 = b_1 = bb2​=b1​=b
